channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In Georgia, two GOP Trump backers vie to face former Atlanta mayor for governor
item.description: Georgia is a swing state where both Democrats and Republicans are deciding the direction their parties will take in the fall in races for the senate and the governor's mansion.
item.content:encoded:
Georgia is a swing state where both Democrats and Republicans are deciding the direction their parties will take in the fall in races for the senate and the governor's mansion.

(Image credit: Brynn Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kallis

item.title: U.S. government to drop tax claims against Trump in broadening of IRS settlement
item.description: As part of the settlement agreement, the U.S. is "forever barred and precluded" from examining or prosecuting President Trump, his sons and the Trump organization's current tax issues, according to a document posted to the DOJ website.
item.content:encoded:
As part of the settlement agreement, the U.S. is "forever barred and precluded" from examining or prosecuting President Trump, his sons and the Trump organization's current tax issues, according to a document posted to the DOJ website.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Can burying power lines help prevent more power outages?
item.description: Power outages are increasing as climate change drives more severe weather events across the U-S. Lines buried UNDERGROUND are significantly more reliable than overhead power lines. But only about a fifth of the country's lines are buried. In northern Michigan, some utilities are trying to change that.
item.content:encoded: Power outages are increasing as climate change drives more severe weather events across the U-S. Lines buried UNDERGROUND are significantly more reliable than overhead power lines. But only about a fifth of the country's lines are buried. In northern Michigan, some utilities are trying to change that.
item.dc:creator: Vivian La

item.title: Minnesota becomes first state to ban prediction markets
item.description: While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.
item.content:encoded:
While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.

(Image credit: Steve Karnowski)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: States sue over new student loan limits on certain nursing and healthcare degrees
item.description: New York, Arizona, North Carolina, Kentucky and Nevada are among the states challenging a rule that limits federal student loans for graduate degrees in nursing, physical therapy and more.
item.content:encoded:
New York, Arizona, North Carolina, Kentucky and Nevada are among the states challenging a rule that limits federal student loans for graduate degrees in nursing, physical therapy and more.

(Image credit: kieferpix)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: The missing men of the American marriage market
item.description: A new study suggests the growing educational and economic divide between men and women is reshaping marriage and family life in America — leaving many women with a shrinking pool of economically stable partners.
item.content:encoded: A new study suggests the growing educational and economic divide between men and women is reshaping marriage and family life in America — leaving many women with a shrinking pool of economically stable partners.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: NTSB hearing will probe cause of fiery UPS jet crash that killed 15 in Louisville
item.description: The nation's top safety investigators will launch a two-day hearing beginning Tuesday into what caused a UPS cargo plane to crash shortly after takeoff in Louisville last year, killing 15 people.
item.content:encoded:
The nation's top safety investigators will launch a two-day hearing beginning Tuesday into what caused a UPS cargo plane to crash shortly after takeoff in Louisville last year, killing 15 people.

(Image credit: Stephen Cohen)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: These men voted for President Trump. They have very different views of how he's doing
item.description: Two Black men from Georgia who voted for President Trump in 2024 have very different views of how the country is doing now, in the first installment of Swing Shift from NPR's Tamara Keith.
item.content:encoded:
Two Black men from Georgia who voted for President Trump in 2024 have very different views of how the country is doing now, in the first installment of Swing Shift from NPR's Tamara Keith.

(Image credit: Illustrations by Tara Anand)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: 'We're not kids anymore': The DACA generation hits their 30s with an unstable future
item.description: Recipients of the Obama-era DACA program are aging, even as the Trump administration moves to weaken the program's protections and benefits.
item.content:encoded:
Recipients of the Obama-era DACA program are aging, even as the Trump administration moves to weaken the program's protections and benefits.

(Image credit: Ash Ponders for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: What we know about how the U.S. government uses spyware (and what we don't)
item.description: Critics of spyware, which can be used to remotely hack into phones, worry the Trump administration is eroding policies that stigmatized the commercial spyware industry.
item.content:encoded:
Critics of spyware, which can be used to remotely hack into phones, worry the Trump administration is eroding policies that stigmatized the commercial spyware industry.

(Image credit: Menahem Kahana)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block
